title: Robotic navigation and transport of objects
abstract: Navigational systems and methods include building a topological graph of an environment using nodes that represent locations in the space and associated directions, with frontiers associated with particular nodes and directions within the topological graph. An action is determined using a policy trained with an action reward function that weighs exploration to find new objects and moving objects to a goal. An agent navigates within the environment in accordance with the determined action.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A navigation method, comprising: building a topological graph of an environment using nodes that represent locations in the space and associated directions, with frontiers associated with particular nodes and directions within the topological graph; determining an action using a policy trained with an action reward function that weighs exploration to find new objects and moving objects to a goal; and navigating an agent within the environment in accordance with the determined action. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein determining the action includes calculating an exploration score that represents a likelihood of finding an object upon exploring a frontier. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein calculating the exploration score includes calculating a respective exploration score for each node-direction combination in the topological graph. 4. The method of claim 2, wherein calculating the exploration score includes processing the topological graph using a graph convolutional neural network. 5. The method of claim 2, wherein calculating the exploration score includes predicting frontier scores on a per-frame basis using a convolutional neural network. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein determining the action selects an action from the group consisting of explore, pickup, and drop and wherein the pickup and drop actions interact with a target object to move the target object from an origin location to a goal location. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the agent is an autonomous device and navigating the agent causes the agent to move within the environment. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the action reward function is expressed as: where is an indicator if an object o was found at a timestep is a reward for finding a new object, is a time penalty for every step that encourages finding objects faster is an indicator if all objects were found, and is an associated success bonus. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein navigating includes using a navigation policy trained with a navigation reward function that weighs distances to objects. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the navigation reward function is expressed as: where is an indicator that a target object has been reached, is the success reward if the agent reaches closer than a threshold distance from the target obj ect is a constant time penalty for each step, is the decrease in geodesic distance with the target object and is a penalty for collision with an object in the environment. 11. A navigation system, comprising: a hardware processor; and a memory to store a computer program that, when executed by the hardware processor, causes the hardware processor to: build a topological graph of an environment using nodes that represent locations in the space and associated directions, with frontiers associated with particular nodes and directions within the topological graph; determine an action using a policy trained with an action reward function that weighs exploration to find new objects and moving objects to a goal; and navigate an agent within the environment in accordance with the determined action. 12. The system of claim 11, wherein the computer program further causes the hardware processor to calculate an exploration score that represents a likelihood of finding an object upon exploring a frontier. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein the calculation of the exploration score includes a respective exploration score for each node-direction combination in the topological graph. 14. The system of claim 12, wherein the calculation of the exploration score includes a graph convolutional neural network to process the topological graph. 15. The system of claim 12, wherein the calculation of the exploration score includes a convolutional neural network to predict frontier scores on a per-frame basis using. 16. The system of claim 11, wherein the computer program further causes the hardware processor to select an action from the group consisting of explore, pickup, and drop, and wherein the pickup and drop actions interact with a target object to move the target object from an origin location to a goal location. 17. The system of claim 11, wherein the agent is an autonomous device and navigating the agent causes the agent to move within the environment. 18. The system of claim 11, wherein the action reward function is expressed as: wher is an indicator if an object o was found at a timestep is a reward for finding a new object is a time penalty for every step that encourages finding objects faster, is an indicator if all objects were found, and is an associated success bonus. 19. The system of claim 11, wherein navigating includes using a navigation policy trained with a navigation reward function that weighs distances to objects. 20. The system of claim 19, wherein the navigation reward function is expressed as: where is an indicator that a target object has been reached, is the success reward if the agent reaches closer than a threshold distance from the target obj ect is a constant time penalty for each step, is the decrease in geodesic distance with the target object and n is a penalty for collision with an object in the environment.
